description: Materials knowledge for ironworkers and erection crews — structural steel shapes, high-strength bolts, anchor bolts, welding consumables, coatings, grating, handrail, and miscellaneous metals used in industrial construction.

W_beams.description: Wide flange beams. Most common structural member.
W_beams.designation: W followed by nominal depth x weight per foot. Example: W12x26 = 12 inch deep, 26 lbs/ft.
W_beams.grades: A992 (50 ksi yield) is standard for W shapes. Replaced A36 (36 ksi) for most applications.

HSS.description: Hollow Structural Sections — round, square, or rectangular tubes.
HSS.designation: HSS followed by dimensions. Round: HSS6.625x0.250 (OD x wall). Rect: HSS8x4x3/8 (depth x width x wall).
HSS.grades: A500 Grade B (46 ksi) or Grade C (50 ksi). ASTM A1085 for improved properties.

angles.designation: L followed by leg lengths x thickness. Example: L4x4x3/8.

channels.designation: C or MC followed by depth x weight. Example: C10x20.

plates.description: Flat stock. Base plates, gussets, stiffeners, connection plates.
plates.grades: A36 (36 ksi), A572 Gr 50 (50 ksi). Use grade matching the connected member.

pipe.grades: A53 Gr B for standard, A106 Gr B for high-temperature service.

structural_steel.identification: Steel members should have mill markings (heat number, grade). For unmarked steel, PMI (Positive Material Identification) may be required before welding.

A325.description: Standard high-strength structural bolt. 120 ksi minimum tensile.
A325.head_marking: A325 and manufacturer mark on head. Three radial lines.
A325.use: Most structural connections in buildings and industrial structures.
A325.installation: Snug-tight, pretensioned, or slip-critical depending on connection type.
A325.reuse: A325 bolts CAN be reused if not damaged. Inspect threads and head.

A490.description: Higher strength structural bolt. 150 ksi minimum tensile.
A490.head_marking: A490 on head. Not interchangeable with A325.
A490.use: Where A325 is insufficient — heavy connections, moment connections.
A490.reuse: A490 bolts SHALL NOT be reused (RCSC specification). One-time use only.
A490.note: A490 is more brittle than A325. Do not over-tighten. Galvanizing A490 is prohibited (hydrogen embrittlement risk).

TC_bolts.description: Tension Control bolt — has a splined end that shears off at the specified tension.
TC_bolts.advantage: No torque wrench needed. Electric shear wrench shears the spline at correct tension. Faster installation.
TC_bolts.inspection: Sheared spline = correct tension achieved. If spline doesn't shear, bolt is not tensioned — reject.
TC_bolts.practical_note: TC bolts are the fastest way to achieve pretensioned bolts in the field. Preferred for large connection bolt-up.

DTI_washers.description: Direct Tension Indicator — washer with bumps that flatten under bolt tension.
DTI_washers.use: Verification of bolt tension. Place under bolt head or nut.
DTI_washers.inspection: Measure gap with feeler gauge. When bumps are compressed to specified gap (typically 0.005 inch), tension is achieved.
DTI_washers.practical_note: DTI washers plus feeler gauge is the most reliable field verification of bolt tension.

installation_methods.snug_tight: Tighten with a few impacts of an impact wrench or full effort with a spud wrench. Plies in firm contact.

pretensioned.turn_of_nut: Snug-tight, then turn nut: 1/3 turn for bolt length up to 4D, 1/2 turn for 4D-8D, 2/3 turn for 8D-12D.
pretensioned.calibrated_wrench: Torque wrench calibrated daily with Skidmore-Wilhelm bolt tension calibrator. Apply torque to specified value.
pretensioned.TC_bolt: Use shear wrench — tighten until spline shears off.
pretensioned.DTI: Tighten until DTI gap meets specification.

installation_methods.slip_critical: Same tensioning as pretensioned, but faying surfaces must meet Class A or B slip coefficient. No paint between plies unless qualified.
installation_methods.bolt_lot_testing: Before installation, test a sample from each bolt lot in a Skidmore-Wilhelm to verify tension vs turns/torque relationship. Document results.

cast_in_place.J_bolt: J-shaped, cast into concrete during pour. Most common for equipment and columns.
cast_in_place.L_bolt: L-shaped. Used where J-bolt hook interferes with rebar.
cast_in_place.headed_anchor: Straight bolt with hex head or plate washer at bottom. Best pullout resistance.
cast_in_place.template: Use template from equipment drawing to set anchor bolt pattern before concrete pour. Check pattern BEFORE concrete sets — you cannot move them after.
cast_in_place.projection: Verify projection above concrete matches equipment drawing. Too short = cannot get nut on. Too long = thread damage.

post_installed.epoxy_anchor: Drilled hole, filled with epoxy (Hilti HIT-RE 500 V3, Simpson SET-3G), anchor rod inserted. Wait for cure before loading.
post_installed.expansion_anchor: Mechanical wedge expansion (Hilti HSL-3, Kwik Bolt). Immediate loading but lower capacity than epoxy in cracked concrete.
post_installed.undercut_anchor: Drilled and undercut hole creates mechanical interlock. Hilti HDA. Highest capacity post-installed anchor.

anchor_bolts.materials: F1554 Grade 36, 55, or 105. Grade 105 for heavy anchor bolts. A193 B7 for high-temperature applications. Galvanized where exposed to weather.
anchor_bolts.common_mistake: Not checking anchor bolt pattern and elevation BEFORE concrete pour. Once concrete is set, you are drilling, and drilled anchors have lower capacity than cast-in-place.

E7018.use: THE standard structural welding electrode. All position.
E7018.storage: Must be kept dry. Open rods: use within 4 hours or re-dry at 500-800F for 1 hour. Rod oven required.

E6010.use: Root passes in pipe, not typically used in structural (lower toughness than 7018).

ER70S_6.use: Shop welding of structural steel. Fast deposition.

E71T_1.use: Field structural welding. High deposition rate. CO2 or 75/25 Ar/CO2 shielding gas.
E71T_1.advantage: Faster than 7018 SMAW. Better wind tolerance than GMAW. Most common field structural process.

E71T_8.use: Field welding where wind prevents gas-shielded processes. No external shielding gas needed.
E71T_8.limitation: Lower toughness than gas-shielded. Some projects restrict use.

surface_prep.SSPC_SP6: Commercial blast cleaning. Removes most mill scale, rust, old coating. 66% clean.
surface_prep.SSPC_SP10: Near-white blast cleaning. 95% clean. Standard for industrial steel.
surface_prep.SSPC_SP5: White metal blast. 100% clean. For immersion service or severe environments.

paint_systems.shop_primer: Inorganic zinc (IOZ) or organic zinc-rich primer. Applied in fabrication shop. 3-5 mils DFT.
paint_systems.intermediate: Epoxy intermediate coat. 4-8 mils DFT. Applied over primer.
paint_systems.finish: Polyurethane topcoat for UV resistance and color. 2-4 mils DFT. Or epoxy for chemical resistance.
paint_systems.fireproofing: Cementitious or intumescent fireproofing on structural members per fire rating requirements. Thickness per engineering.

coatings.field_touch_up: After erection, all bolted connections, welds, and damaged areas need touch-up coating. Match existing system — primer + intermediate + finish.
coatings.DFT_measurement: Dry Film Thickness measured with magnetic gauge (Positector, Elcometer). Verify each coat meets specification range.

bar_grating.types: Welded (W), press-locked (P), swage-locked. Carbon steel A1011, galvanized, aluminum, stainless.
bar_grating.bearing_bar: 1 inch, 1-1/4 inch, or 1-1/2 inch depth. Spacing: 15/16 inch or 1-3/16 inch center-to-center.
bar_grating.fastening: Saddle clips, bolt-down clips, welded. Clips preferred for removable grating.

handrail.OSHA_requirements: 42 inch top rail, mid-rail at 21 inches, toe board 4 inches minimum height. Must withstand 200 lb top rail load.
handrail.types: Pipe rail (1-1/2 inch pipe), Unistrut rail, prefabricated industrial rail.

grating_and_misc.stair_treads: Grating tread, checkered plate (diamond plate), or grip strut. Anti-slip nosing on front edge.
grating_and_misc.checkered_plate: A36 steel with raised diamond pattern. Floor plate for platforms, covers, walkways. Minimum 1/4 inch thick.
